title: Heatwave conditions continue in Vidarbha as Akola and Amravati record over 45 degrees Celsius; Mumbai reels under hot weather - Mid-day
search_term: heatwave (Ahmednagar OR Akola OR Amravati OR Aurangabad OR Beed OR Bhandara OR Buldhana OR Chandrapur OR Dhule OR Gadchiroli OR Gondia OR Hingoli OR Jalgaon OR Jalna OR Kolhapur OR Latur OR "Mumbai City" OR "Mumbai Suburban" OR Nagpur OR Nanded OR Nandurbar OR Nashik OR Osmanabad OR Palghar OR Parbhani OR Pune OR Raigad OR Ratnagiri OR Sangli OR Satara OR Sindhudurg OR Solapur OR Thane OR Wardha OR Washim OR Yavatmal) Maharashtra
full_text:
Mumbai continued to witness hot weather conditions on Tuesday. Representational Pic/File
Buldhana and Brahmpuri both recorded 43.5 degrees Celsius, while Nagpur touched 43 degrees Celsius. Chandrapur recorded 42.2 degrees Celsius and Yavatmal reported 41.8 degrees Celsius, the weather department data stated.
Minimum temperatures also remained high across Vidarbha, increasing discomfort during the night hours.
Akola recorded a minimum temperature of 29.1 degrees Celsius, among the highest in the region. Buldhana reported 28.8 degrees Celsius, while Chandrapur and Wardha both registered minimum temperatures of 28 degrees Celsius, it said.
Weather officials said the combination of high daytime temperatures and warm nights is contributing to heat stress in several districts.
Mumbai also witnessed hot weather conditions, although temperatures remained lower than those recorded in Vidarbha.
Among suburban weather stations, Ram Mandir recorded the highest maximum temperature at 37.9 degrees Celsius. Bandra recorded 35.6 degrees Celsius, followed by Vikhroli at 35.5 degrees Celsius. Vidyavihar reported 34.9 degrees Celsius, while Dahisar recorded 34.3 degrees Celsius.
Meanwhile, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) had said in its monthly forecast that above-normal heatwave days are likely in some parts of the foothills of the Himalayas, east coast states, Gujarat and Maharashtra in May.
The department also highlighted that the rainfall averaged over the country as a whole during May is most likely to be above normal, with the onset of the southwest monsoon over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands expected around May 14-16.
Director General of Meteorology, IMD, Mrutyunjay Mohapatra, said, "During May 2026, minimum temperatures are expected to be above normal across many parts of the country."
"However, many areas of northwest India, along with some parts of central India and adjoining areas of peninsular India and southern parts of northeast India, are likely to experience normal to below-normal minimum temperatures," he said in a statement.

title: IMD warns of intense heatwave in Maharashtra, issues yellow alert - News9live
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
full_text:
IMD warns of intense heatwave in Maharashtra, issues yellow alert
According to the weather department, dry weather is expected to continue in most parts of Maharashtra, IMD has issued yellow alert for next five day in north and Central Maharashtra.
New Delhi: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued an warning stating that Mumbai and several parts of Maharashtra are likely to face heatwave-like conditions for the next three days. The weather agency said temperatures are expected to rise gradually in the state till May 15.
According to the weather department, dry weather is expected to continue in most parts of Maharashtra. The IMD has also issued yellow alert for next five day in north and Central Maharashtra.
Coastal and inland Districts on alert
As per the department, Mumbai, Thane, Raigad and Ratnagiri are expected to see hot and humid weather. The report also warns that parts of Madhya Maharashtra and Marathwada may face heatwave conditions in some areas.
Districts like Dhule, Jalgaon, Nashik, Nandurbar, Pune, and Ahilyanagar will probably face heatwave conditions till May 14. Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Beed, Jalna, Hingoli, Parbhani and Nanded might also experience heatwave conditions from May 12 and 15.
Vidarbha bears the brunt
The region of Vidarbha has received a strongest warning. As per this, Akola may face heatwave conditions all five days. Amravati and Wardha could also see heatwaves later this week. Nagpur, Chandrapur, Gondia and several other districts have “No Warning” as of now.
The temperatures across several parts of Maharashtra will stay above normal, with North Madhya Maharashtra recording highest daytime temperatures between 41 degrees Celsius and 43 degrees Celsius. North Konkan, including Mumbai and near districts, likely record maximum 35 to 39 degrees Celsius, which is above normal. The minimum temperatures in the region stay between 26 to 30 degrees Celsius. Doctors have advised people to avoid going out in direct sunlight during peak hours. Temperatures in several areas are already above normal.

title: Nagpur Temperatures Likely to Rise by 2–4°C; IMD Issues Yellow Alert Across Vidarbha - Nagpur Today
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Maharashtra
full_text:
Nagpur: After a brief spell of relief, intense summer heat has returned to Nagpur and several parts of Vidarbha, with soaring temperatures and harsh sunlight once again troubling residents. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a Yellow Alert for Nagpur and multiple districts across the region, warning of a further rise in temperatures over the coming days.
Over the past few days, rainfall triggered by cyclonic activity had brought down temperatures across Vidarbha and other parts of Maharashtra. However, with skies now clearing, heat conditions have intensified rapidly.
Temperature May Rise by 2–4 Degrees Celsius
According to the weather department, temperatures in several Vidarbha districts are expected to rise by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius during the next week, while some areas could witness an increase of up to 4 degrees Celsius.
The IMD has specifically warned that temperatures in districts such as Wardha, Akola, and Amravati could cross the 46-degree Celsius mark. In view of the severe heat conditions, a Yellow Alert has been issued for these regions.
Cyclonic Systems Impacting Vidarbha Weather
Meteorologists said that moisture-laden winds from the Andaman Sea and Arabian Sea had earlier helped keep temperatures under control. However, that effect has now weakened.
At the same time, cyclonic systems developing over Gujarat and Rajasthan are influencing weather patterns in Vidarbha, contributing to the steady rise in temperatures across the region.
Authorities have advised residents to avoid direct exposure to the sun during peak afternoon hours, stay hydrated, and take necessary precautions against heat-related illnesses.

title: Maharashtra Weather Alert For May 13: IMD Issues Yellow Alert for 12 Districts as Heatwave Intensifies - PUNE PULSE
full_text:
Maharashtra, May 12, 2026: Heatwave conditions across Maharashtra are expected to intensify further on Wednesday, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing a yellow alert for 12 districts in the state. Clear skies and rising daytime temperatures are likely to worsen heat conditions across several regions, officials said.
The IMD has issued a heatwave warning for Dhule, Nandurbar, Jalgaon, Nashik, Ahilyanagar, Pune, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Jalna, Parbhani, Beed, Akola, and Amravati for May 13. Most parts of Maharashtra are expected to witness dry and hot weather, especially during afternoon hours when temperatures are likely to peak.
In the Konkan region, including Mumbai, Thane, Raigad, Ratnagiri, and Sindhudurg, humid weather conditions are expected to continue. Minimum temperatures in these coastal districts may remain between 26 and 28 degrees Celsius, while maximum temperatures could touch 33 to 35 degrees Celsius. High humidity levels are likely to make conditions more uncomfortable for residents.
Western Maharashtra, including Pune, Satara, Sangli, Kolhapur, and Solapur, is also witnessing a sharp rise in temperatures. Pune and Solapur are expected to record maximum temperatures between 42 and 43 degrees Celsius. Minimum temperatures in the region may remain between 22 and 24 degrees Celsius, with intense heat likely during the afternoon.
North Maharashtra districts such as Dhule, Nandurbar, Jalgaon, and Nashik are likely to continue experiencing severe heatwave conditions. Jalgaon and Dhule may record temperatures above 42 degrees Celsius, while Nashik is expected to witness dry and hot weather throughout the day.
In the Marathwada region, districts including Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Jalna, Parbhani, and Beed are likely to see maximum temperatures ranging from 41 to 43 degrees Celsius. Dry weather conditions are expected to increase the impact of the heatwave across the region.
Vidarbha districts such as Akola, Amravati, Nagpur, Wardha, and Yavatmal are also expected to remain under intense heat conditions. Akola and Amravati may witness temperatures between 44 and 45 degrees Celsius, while Nagpur could record maximum temperatures between 40 and 42 degrees Celsius.
The IMD has advised residents to avoid unnecessary travel during afternoon hours, stay hydrated, and take precautions against heatstroke as hot and dry weather is expected to continue across most parts of Maharashtra.
